requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must properly structure health insurance premium payments through employer cafeteria plan or self-employed health insurance deduction to maximize pre-tax benefits and reduce overall tax liability
criteria: Employees: Premiums paid through employer Section 125 cafeteria plan deducted pre-tax from wages
Self-employed: Can deduct health insurance premiums as above-the-line deduction reducing adjusted gross income
Self-employed deduction limited to net self-employment income from business under which plan established
Cannot deduct insurance premiums for months eligible for employer-sponsored plan (including spouse's plan)
Qualifying insurance: medical, dental, vision, qualified long-term care (subject to age-based limits)
S Corporation 2%+ shareholders treated as self-employed for health insurance deduction purposes
Deduction reduces income tax but not self-employment tax for self-employed individuals
Premiums for coverage of self, spouse, dependents, and children under age 27 (even if not dependent) qualify

health_insurance_premium_rules.employee_cafeteria_plan: Pre-tax treatment through Section 125 reduces wages for income tax and FICA purposes
health_insurance_premium_rules.self_employed_deduction: Above-the-line deduction on Schedule 1; reduces AGI but not self-employment income/tax
health_insurance_premium_rules.income_limitation: Self-employed deduction cannot exceed net self-employment income from that business
health_insurance_premium_rules.s_corp_2_percent: Premiums paid by S Corp for 2%+ shareholders treated as wages then deductible by shareholder
health_insurance_premium_rules.medicare_premium_limitation: Medicare premiums generally deductible; Medicare supplement plans qualify
health_insurance_premium_rules.ltc_premium_limits: Long-term care insurance premiums deductible up to age-based limits ($470-$5,880 for 2025)

restrictions: Cannot deduct premiums as self-employed if eligible for employer plan even if choose not to participate
Spouse's employer coverage eligibility disqualifies self-employed deduction for that coverage period
Premium tax credit (marketplace subsidy) reduces or eliminates self-employed deduction for same premiums
S Corp 2%+ shareholder premiums must be included in W-2 wages then deducted by shareholder
Partnership guaranteed payments treatment differs; health insurance may be part of guaranteed payment
Self-employed deduction cannot create or increase net operating loss
Medicare Part B and D premiums deductible but cannot create excess deduction beyond income
State tax treatment may differ from federal requiring separate analysis and state return adjustments
